title: Credential Pools
summary: Credential pools let you register multiple API keys or OAuth tokens for the same provider; Hermes automatically rotates to the next healthy key when one hits rate limits or billing quotas, providing same-provider key rotation before cross-provider fallback activates.

cli_commands.purpose: Add a credential (prompts for type and key)
Add an OAuth credential via browser login

config_keys.description: Rotation strategy per provider: fill_first | round_robin | least_used | random

code_examples.caption: Add Anthropic OAuth credential
code_examples.code:
hermes auth add anthropic --type oauth
# Opens browser for OAuth login

gotchas: 429 rate limit: retry same key once (transient), then rotate — 1 hour cooldown
402 billing error: immediately rotate — 24 hour cooldown
401 auth expired: try token refresh first, rotate only if refresh fails
Auto-seeded entries (from env vars) are auto-pruned if the env var is removed; manual entries are never auto-pruned
Thread safety: pool uses a threading lock for all state mutations to support concurrent gateway sessions
Credential pools are tried first — only after all pool keys are exhausted does fallback_model activate
full_text:
# Credential Pools

Credential pools let you register multiple API keys or OAuth tokens for the same provider. When one key hits a rate limit or billing quota, Hermes automatically rotates to the next healthy key.
